description:
A backup engineer wants to make sure last night’s archive has not been corrupted in transit.  
  1. A small internal status page already exists at the path  
     file:///home/user/server/backup_report.html.  
     Inside that HTML page there is exactly one SHA-256 hash enclosed by <code> … </code> tags.  
  2. The physical archive that must be validated is stored in  
     /home/user/data/backup.tar.gz.  

  Your assignment is to verify that the hash published in the HTML page matches the
  real SHA-256 checksum of the archive and to write a short text report that the
  automated grader can parse.

  Expected end-state
  ------------------
  A new file called  
      /home/user/backup_checks/verification.log  
  must exist.  The file must contain **exactly three newline-terminated lines**
  in the following strict format (note the single space after each colon and the
  requirement for lower-case “true” or “false”):

      expected: <hash-from-html>
      actual: <hash-of-/home/user/data/backup.tar.gz>
      match: <true-or-false>

  • <hash-from-html> must be the 64-character hexadecimal value extracted from the
    <code> … </code> element inside
    /home/user/server/backup_report.html.  
  • <hash-of-/home/user/data/backup.tar.gz> must be the 64-character hexadecimal
    SHA-256 digest you calculate directly from the archive file.  
  • <true-or-false> must be “true” (lower-case) if and only if the two hashes are
    identical; otherwise it must be “false”.

  Notes & constraints
  -------------------
  • Treat the HTML page exactly as a remote site would be scraped: do not open it
    in a GUI browser—“headless” tools such as curl, wget, or any other command-line
    fetcher/​parser are expected.  
  • All work must be done with ordinary user privileges.  
  • Create the directory /home/user/backup_checks if it does not already exist
    so that verification.log can be written there.  
  • No extra output, comments, or blank lines should appear in the log file—only
    the three specified lines.  
  • Finish by saving the file; the grader will read the log to decide success.
truth:
Nightly Backup Report

Backup Verification

The SHA256 checksum for the latest tarball is: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

]]>
